_description: Business Rules Engine (BRE) — Defines table purposes, column metadata, inter-table relationships, and formula/derivation logic. This file is the single source of truth for understanding the database schema from a business perspective.

sch_single_data_sheet1.purpose: Manufacturing quality-control tracking data for a production line. Each row represents one process step within a batch on a specific date, recording how many units were input, passed first-time, reworked, or scrapped, along with defects found.
sch_single_data_sheet1.granularity: One row per batch × process step × date

batch_id.description: Unique identifier for the production batch (e.g. B001, B002).

batch_id_step.description: Sequential step number within the batch — indicates the order in which process steps occur (1 = first step, 2 = second step, etc.).

batch_id_unique.description: A composite fraction identifier representing the batch's proportional step position. Derived as batch_id_step divided by total steps in the batch.

formula.description: Represents the relative position of this step within the batch process. E.g. step 1 of 4 = 0.25.

date.description: The calendar date when this production step was recorded. Stored in ISO format YYYY-MM-DD.

day.description: Day of the week name derived from the date column.

formula.description: Derived from the date column — the full name of the weekday (Monday, Tuesday, etc.).

day_type.description: Indicates if the date is a Working day or Holiday for the factory.

week.description: ISO week number of the year derived from the date column.

formula.description: ISO week number (1–53) computed from the date.

day_class.description: Whether the date falls on a Weekday or Weekend.

formula.expression: IF day IN (Saturday, Sunday) THEN 'Weekend' ELSE 'Weekday'
formula.description: Classifies the day as Weekend (Sat/Sun) or Weekday (Mon–Fri).

month.description: Full month name derived from the date column.

formula.description: Full month name derived from date (January, February, …, December).

product.description: The product being manufactured in this batch (e.g. A_Motor, B_Pump).

line.description: The production line on which this batch runs (e.g. L1, L2).

process_step.description: The name of the manufacturing process step. Each batch passes through a sequence of steps.
process_step.notes: Values are case-sensitive. The typical production flow is: Cutting → Assembly → Welding → Testing.

process_step_2.description: Numeric code for the process step — maps 1:1 with process_step. Cutting=1, Assembly=2, Welding=3, Testing=4.

units_input.description: Total number of units that entered this process step for quality checking.

units_passed_first_time.description: Number of units that passed quality check on the first attempt without any rework.

units_passed_first_time_2.description: First-Time Pass Rate — the ratio of units that passed first time to units input. A key quality KPI.

formula.sql: CAST(units_passed_first_time AS REAL) / CAST(units_input AS REAL)
formula.description: First Pass Yield (FPY) = units_passed_first_time ÷ units_input. Measures the proportion of units that pass without rework. Higher is better. Target: > 0.95 (95%).
formula.outputFormat: ratio (0 to 1, multiply by 100 for percentage)

units_passed_first_time_class.description: Classification of the first-time pass rate into quality tiers.

formula.expression: IF FPY >= 0.97 THEN 'P3 Best' ELIF FPY >= 0.95 THEN 'P2 Observe' ELIF FPY >= 0.90 THEN 'P1 Attention' ELSE 'P0 Critical'
formula.description: Categorises First Pass Yield into priority bands: P3 Best (≥97%), P2 Observe (95–97%), P1 Attention (90–95%), P0 Critical (<90%).

units_reworked_and_passed.description: Number of units that initially failed but were reworked and then passed quality check.

units_reworked_and_passed_2.description: Rework Pass Rate — ratio of reworked-and-passed units to total units input.

formula.sql: CAST(units_reworked_and_passed AS REAL) / CAST(units_input AS REAL)
formula.description: Rework Rate = units_reworked_and_passed ÷ units_input. Lower is better — high values indicate quality issues that require rework.

units_reworked_and_passed_class.description: Classification of the rework rate into priority bands.

formula.expression: IF rework_rate <= 0.02 THEN 'P3 Best' ELIF <= 0.03 THEN 'P2 Observe' ELIF <= 0.05 THEN 'P1 Attention' ELSE 'P0 Critical'
formula.description: Categorises rework rate: P3 Best (≤2%), P2 Observe (2–3%), P1 Attention (3–5%), P0 Critical (>5%). Lower rework is better.

units_scrapped.description: Total number of units that were permanently rejected / scrapped at this process step. These units cannot be reworked.

units_scrapped_1.description: Scrap Rate (relative to passed units) — ratio of scrapped units to units_passed_first_time.

formula.sql: CAST(units_scrapped AS REAL) / CAST(units_passed_first_time AS REAL)
formula.description: Scrap rate relative to first-time-passed units. Measures scrap severity against good output.

units_scrapped_class1.description: Classification of scrap rate (vs passed) into priority bands.

units_scrapped_2.description: Scrap Rate (relative to total input) — ratio of scrapped units to total units input. Primary scrap KPI.

formula.sql: CAST(units_scrapped AS REAL) / CAST(units_input AS REAL)
formula.description: Scrap Rate = units_scrapped ÷ units_input. The primary scrap KPI. Lower is better. Target: < 0.02 (2%).

units_scrapped_class2.description: Classification of scrap rate (vs input) into priority bands.

formula.expression: IF scrap_rate <= 0.01 THEN 'P3 Best' ELIF <= 0.02 THEN 'P2 Observe' ELIF <= 0.03 THEN 'P1 Attention' ELSE 'P0 Critical'
formula.description: Categorises scrap rate: P3 Best (≤1%), P2 Observe (1–2%), P1 Attention (2–3%), P0 Critical (>3%).

defects_found.description: Total number of quality defects found during inspection at this process step. One unit can have multiple defects.

check1.description: Validation/check flag column. Typically 0 or 1 indicating whether a post-process quality check was performed.

businessRules.name: Unit Conservation
businessRules.description: For each row, the total units accounted for must balance: units_passed_first_time + units_reworked_and_passed + units_scrapped should approximately equal units_input.

businessRules.name: Process Step Order
businessRules.description: Each batch follows a fixed process flow: Cutting (step 1) → Assembly (step 2) → Welding (step 3) → Testing (step 4). batch_id_step indicates the order.

businessRules.name: Scrap is Terminal
businessRules.description: Scrapped units are permanently lost and cannot be reworked. They are removed from the production line.

businessRules.name: Classification Tiers
businessRules.description: All classification columns (_class, _class1, _class2) use a 4-tier priority system: P0 Critical (worst), P1 Attention, P2 Observe, P3 Best (best). These are used for quality alerts and dashboards.

businessRules.name: Date Derivations
businessRules.description: Columns day, week, month, and day_class are all derived from the date column. They are pre-computed during Excel import and should not be independently modified.

kpis.name: First Pass Yield (FPY)
kpis.description: Percentage of units that pass quality on the first attempt. The #1 quality metric.

kpis.name: Scrap Rate
kpis.description: Percentage of units permanently rejected. Lower is better.

kpis.name: Rework Rate
kpis.description: Percentage of units that needed rework before passing. Indicates hidden quality costs.

kpis.name: Defect Density
kpis.description: Average defects per unit input. Can exceed 1.0 if units have multiple defects.

relationships.description: Currently the system has a single denormalised table (sch_single_data_sheet1) containing all dimensions and measures. As new Excel files or tables are added, define relationships here.

glossary.FPY: First Pass Yield — percentage of units passing quality on the first attempt.
glossary.Scrap: Units permanently rejected that cannot be reworked or salvaged.
glossary.Rework: Units that initially failed quality but were repaired and passed on a second check.
glossary.Batch: A group of units produced together in one production run.
glossary.Process Step: A stage in the manufacturing pipeline (Cutting → Assembly → Welding → Testing).
glossary.KPI: Key Performance Indicator — a measurable value showing production effectiveness.
glossary.P0 Critical: Worst quality classification tier — requires immediate action.
glossary.P3 Best: Excellent quality — meets or exceeds targets.
